article_meta.title: close Video Supporting Jasmine Crockett for Texas Senate is a 'waste of money,' comedian says 'Fox News Saturday Night'

reasoning: The model commits multiple attribution rule violations by flagging quoted text from RNC spokesman Zach Kraft (items 2 and 3) and paraphrased/quoted text from Gene Wu (item 5) as author bias. Item 1 partially flags author prose but includes the quoted phrase 'wokeness at its worst' which is attributed to Republicans. Item 4 is the most defensible - it identifies genuine author-level framing choices. The model also misses some author-level bias like the loaded language 'Heated racial rhetoric in Texas is flaring' (author's own sensational framing) and 'Racially focused flare-ups have broken out' as author prose word choices, though it partially captures the latter in item 4.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 2 and 3 are direct quotes from RNC spokesman Zach Kraft, clearly inside quotation marks. Item 5 flags paraphrased/quoted content from Gene Wu. These are clear attribution rule violations. Item 1 mixes author prose with an attributed Republican phrase. Only item 4 is purely about author editorial choices.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 2, 3, and 5 are false positives because they flag quoted/attributed source language. Item 1 is partially a false positive due to including the attributed phrase. Item 4 is the most defensible genuine author-level bias finding. So roughly 3-4 out of 5 items are problematic.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model partially captures the author's framing choices (item 4) but misses specific author-prose bias instances like 'Heated racial rhetoric in Texas is flaring' as loaded word choice, 'fiery response' as a subjective qualifying adjective, and the overall structural slant of the article which heavily features Republican criticism without equivalent Democratic defense. Some genuine instances were caught but others were missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Item 4 (Bias by Story Choice and Placement) is correctly labeled. Item 1's 'Slant' label is defensible for the author's framing portion. The labels on the attribution-violating items (2, 3, 5) are moot since they shouldn't have been flagged. The Elite vs. Populist Bias label on item 5 is a stretch even if it weren't an attribution violation.
